program.name: Low Income Home Energy Assistance Program (LIHEAP)
program.description:
LIHEAP helps Texas families with low incomes pay their energy bills. This includes heating and cooling costs. You do not have to pay back this help.

Money goes to your utility provider — not to you as cash. The amount depends on your household income and size.

Older adults, people with disabilities, and families with young children get help first. To qualify, you must pay for your own energy — either on your own or as part of your rent. Got a shutoff notice or past-due energy bill? You may qualify for extra crisis help. HVAC repair or replacement help may also be available.

To apply, click 'Apply Now' or call 2-1-1 to find your local agency. Funding runs out each year, so apply as early in the year as you can.
program.description_short: Help paying home energy bills for low-income Texas families
program.website_description: Help paying home energy bills for low-income Texas families

warning_message.message: Funding is limited and this program may not be accepting applications at all times. Call 2-1-1 or visit the TDHCA website to confirm your local agency is currently open.

documents.text: Recent utility bills (electric, gas, or propane) for the past 12 months

navigators.name: 2-1-1 Texas
navigators.description: 2-1-1 Texas connects residents with local health and human services resources. Call 2-1-1 (press Option 1) to find the Comprehensive Energy Assistance Program (CEAP/LIHEAP) provider in your area.
